titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Vic Mizzy Papers PASC-M.0144
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Caroline Cubé.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2022 January 20.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-20 14:33:46 -0800.

did.abstract: The collection consists of manuscript and reproduction scores by composer Vic Mizzy for television series (including the Addams Family and Green Acres), made-for-television movies, and films, as well as manuscript songs and published sheet music. Also present are personal papers and correspondence, photographs, audiovisual material (especially audiotapes), books, and artifacts.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Addition to the collection gift of Lynn Mizzy Jonas, 2010.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
The collection is organized into the following series:
Series 1. Scores and related material
Series 2. Songs
Series 3. Personal papers
Series 4. Photographs
Series 5. Audiovisual material
Series 6. Books
Series 7. Artifacts and ephemera

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Composer Vic Mizzy was born in Brooklyn, New York on January 9, 1916. He worked as a songwriter in New York for several decades and moved to Los Angeles in 1963 to write music for television. Mizzy composed the themes and scores for the hit programs The Addams Family, Green Acres, and other sitcoms and made-for-television movies. In addition, he wrote music for a number of films including The Ghost and Mr. Chicken (1966), The Reluctant Astronaut (1967), and Don't Make Waves (1967). Mizzy also contributed music to the Spider-Man 2 (2004) and Spider-Man 3 (2007) films. His first wife was the singer and radio personality Mary Small. Vic Mizzy died in Los Angeles, California on October 17, 2009.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: COLLECTION CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials you must notify the reference desk in advance of your visit.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Vic Mizzy Papers (Collection PASC-M 144). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of manuscript and reproduction scores for television series, made-for-television movies, and films, as well as manuscript songs and published sheet music. Also present are personal papers and correspondence, photographs, audiovisual material (especially audiotapes), books, and artifacts.
Scores and published music for the Addams Family and Green Acres television series are located in Series 1.
This collection contains some material, including correspondence, photographs, clippings, and audiotapes, related to Mizzy's first wife the singer Mary Small and to other family members.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of manuscript and reproduction scores as well as related material for some titles, including cue sheets and scripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript and reproduction scores for main theme and other music for the original series. Published sheet music and instructional arrangements (band, string orchestra, jazz ensemble) for the main theme are also present (box 20, folders 4-6).

did.unittitle: The Double Life of Henry Phyfe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Bulk is reproductions of manuscript scores for individual episodes. Also present is published sheet music for the main theme (box 20).

did.unittitle: The Phyllis Diller Show (The Pruitts of Southampton)
The Shirley Temple Show [Shirley Temple Storybook]
Halloween with the New Addams Family
The Caper of the Golden Bulls
Did You Hear the One about the Traveling Saleslady?
The Shakiest Gun in the West

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Manuscript scores of music by other composers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Songs with music by Mizzy and lyrics by Mann Curtis, Irving Taylor, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten, typewritten, and reproduction versions of songs and lyrics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: A set of lyrics for Mizzy's songs kept separately.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of letters, telegrams, faxes, and cards received, as well as printed email correspondence. Many are fan letters and requests for autographs; also present is correspondence from songwriter and music organizations regarding events and publicity. Telegrams from the 1950s are mostly addressed to Mary Small. In addition, there are some copies/drafts of outgoing letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Partial drafts of scripts for television shows Jury Duty and Something to Sing About by Mizzy and Mann Curtis; also reviews and a draft of Mizzy's So You Want to Compose for TV and the Movies?.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of a c.v. and a soundtrack album license for The Ghost and Mr. Chicken.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of several binders of instructional material by Mizzy and others, all presumably for a course he taught at New York University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes several articles about Mary Small (folder 7).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Photographs are primarily of Vic Mizzy, his first wife the singer Mary Small, their daughters, and other family members. There are also a number of images and stills from film and television productions, including Addams Family and Green Acres, as well as portraits of other entertainers.

Some photographs were originally housed together in albums or files, particularly those that have been labeled "family photographs." The rest of the items were loose in boxes and have been organized according to subject. Oversized photographs are housed separately in box 18.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Mizzy at work as well as stills and set photos from several television series, including Addams Family, Green Acres, Klondike, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Mostly events of songwriters' organizations, especially Songwriters Guild of America. Includes some contact sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Subjects are mostly musicians, also some actors; many are autographed to Mizzy.

did.unittitle: JPEG on floppy disc: "pic of Vic Mizzy"

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Use of electronic format material requires special equipment or further processing may be required for viewing. To review these materials you must notify the reference desk in advance of your visit.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: SERIES CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials you must notify the reference desk in advance of your visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The bulk of audio material is music by Mizzy for television series and films. Also present are a number of recordings of Mizzy's first wife, Mary Small, as well as his daughter, Patty Mizzy. Audiotapes are undated except where noted.

did.unittitle: Subseries 1. Audiotapes, film and television music
The Caper of the Golden Bulls (7") [4 reels]
Did You Hear the One about the Traveling Saleslady? (7") [2 reels]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: One reel shares content with Pioneer Spirit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: 4" reels share content with The Phyllis Diller Show [The Pruitts of Southampton].

did.unittitle: The Double Life of Henry Phyfe (4") [3 reels]
The Ghost and Mr. Chicken (7") [2 reels]
Halloween with the New Addams Family (7") [3 reels]
How to Frame a Figg (7") [2 reels]
The Phyllis Diller Show [The Pruitts of Southampton] (4", 7") [4 reels]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: 4" reels share content with The Don Rickles Show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: One 5" reel shares content with Doc.

did.unittitle: The Shakiest Gun in the West (7") [2 reels]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes Addams Family and Green Acres.

did.unittitle: Subseries 2. Audiotapes, songs and other music
Songs - Vic Mizzy (5", 7") [6 reels] scope note
Songs - Mary Small (7") [2 reels] scope note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: 5": Tracks listed for one reel are: "Patty, You're in Love" and "You Name the Time." Second reel consists of "Patty's review of the movie." 10 1/2": Unison (London) recording with Tommy Britt, 1959.

did.unittitle: Traffic safety promos (5", 16 mm with sound) [14 reels]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Buckle Up Song" and versions of "In the Middle, in the Middle, in the Middle" (one with Patty Mizzy vocals). Also present are PSAs for pedestrian safety for the NYC Dept. of Traffic in several languages.

did.unittitle: The Whole World Is Singing My Song (7") [1 reel] (scope note)
Maestro Amplified Sound System for Woodwinds (7") [1 reel]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes 7", 10", and 12" discs, mostly acetate, of Mizzy's television and film music and songs. Also included are several songs by Mary Small and Patty Mizzy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of television and film music, songs, interviews, events, and music by others; also a few recordings of Mary Small and Patty Mizzy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of television and film music, songs and soundtracks, recordings by others of Mizzy's songs, and interviews; in addition there is a small number of recordings by Mary Small and Patty Mizzy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Bulk is VHS tapes of films with Mizzy's music. Also present are some of Mizzy's television themes, interviews, and events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Most books are autographed to Mizzy.

did.unittitle: Adam, Joey and Henry Tobias. The Borscht Belt.
Diller, Phyllis. The Joys of Aging & How to Avoid Them.
Greenbaum, Everett. The Golden-Berg Who Couldn't Dance.
Murray, Lyn. Musician A Hollywood Journal of Wives, Writers, Lawyers, Directors, Producers and Music.
Shemel, Sidney and M. William Krasilovsky. The Business of Music.
Tobias, Henry. Music in My Heart and Borscht in My Blood.
White, John I. Git Along, Little Dogies Songs and Songmakers of the American West.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Lobby cards for the films Did You Hear the One about the Traveling Saleslady?, Don't Make Waves, How to Frame a Figg, The Night Walker, and The Reluctant Astronaut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: A mixed media portrait of Mizzy and a reproduction Charles Addams Addam's Family cartoon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Includes programs, flyers, stamps, and invitations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Consists of three plaques; two are signage from Mizzy's house, the other a commendation from Variety magazine to Mizzy.
